entry.title: Re: Duplicate Serial Number while doing OBD - PGI
entry.content: With the standard customizing content, you can't receive a material/serial number combination twice. If you'd do so, you'd get a status error, since you can't receive a serial number in status ESTO. In private cloud/OP, you can however change the system status customizing to allow this. You can check this in transaction BS22 => system status I0184/ESTO ==> business transaction PMS1/Goods Receipts.

entry.title: Re: Recommendations for Wrapper Interface Design
entry.content:
Great blog!

Could you please elaborate on some common challenges customers face in projects with wrappers and share some advice:
Who do you recommand to create Wrappers, the people wanting to use the SAP object for the first time? Or some central Team?
How do we make sure the Wrapper gets locked in the proper Transports and is available before depending objects get transported? Who is responsible for the wrapper once something changes in SAP? All users? A central commitee?

If I use the Compatibility of Released API checks, the wrapper needs to be kept stable, but if I use ABAP Cloud I need to create the contract early, to be able to use the Wrapper in ABAP Cloud, which prevents me then from changing it afterwards.
How do I solve that target conflict?

"Define smaller structures: Avoid tight coupling to SAP structures. Rather than reusing or copying large SAP structures, declare smaller, dedicated structures with required fields only. This reduces the dependency footprint."
How do we deal with the challenge of custom fields? If no SAP structures are included, custom fields will not come into the wrapper interface automatically. Even if we add them manually, each custom field then might lead to lots of wrappers needed to be adjusted and transported, etc.
Also don't dedicated structures defeat the purpose of reusable wrappers? Or would you recommend wrapping the same BAPI multiple times for different use-cases?

Thanks for the support.

entry.title: Re: DMEE Payment file format
entry.content:
ISO 20022 – pain.001.001.09: Key Structural Changes and Impact

The pain.001.001.09 message version introduces several structural modifications to the XML schema compared to previous versions (e.g., 001.03 or 001.06), including:

  • New tags and deprecation of obsolete elements.
  • Hierarchical changes (e.g., InstrPrty, SvcLvl, PmtTpInf).
  • Stricter validation rules for fields such as IBAN/BIC formats, dates, and amounts.
  • New fields related to traceability, regulatory reporting, and cross-border payments.

Impact on SAP Systems:
Internal systems for XML generation and parsing must be updated to ensure compliance, including:

  • Mapping adjustments (XSD schemas, field transformations).
  • Enhanced validation logic (unit and integration testing).
  • Testing and adaptation of existing workflows.

Start with KBA 3159759 - CGI: CGI_XML_CT, CGI_XML_DD Format Updates #38 (or more recent ones when available)

entry.title: Re: Automatically transfer text from the purchase order i...
entry.content:
Dear @dnikishaev_1,

The PO item short text is not transferred to the FI document item text by default during goods receipt this is standard behaviour, confirmed in SAP KBA 2921484 (PO short text in Goods Receipt not transferred to FI). However, SAP provides a standard no-development way to make this automatic. In the Post Goods Receipt for Purchasing Document app enter the PO, go to item level, open General Information -> Settings and activate Default Setting for item Short text. This is a one-time, per-user setting once set the PO item short text (EKPO-TXZ01) is automatically copied into the journal entry line item text (SGTXT) on every subsequent goods receipt for that user

If you need this independently of the per-user setting e.g. for MIGO/Web GUI or API-driven postings or applied for all users you can implement it via released extensibility BAdI FIN_CODING_BLOCK_SUBSTITUTION (Coding Block Substitution) reading the short text from released CDS view I_PurchaseOrderItemAPI01 and populating SGTXT

More details below:

SAP KBA 2921484 – PO short text in Goods Receipt not transferred to FI
SAP Help – Business Add-Ins for Substitution for Journal Entries
SAP Help – Purchase Order Item / I_PurchaseOrderItemAPI01

So the requirement is achievable in Public Cloud first try the standard Default Setting for item Short text user parameter, only if that does not cover your scenario, fall back to the released substitution BAdI

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: SAP Business One Down Payment Reconciliation Error
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am facing an issue in SAP Business One regarding internal reconciliation between A/P Invoices and a Vendor Down Payment.

Scenario:

  • SAP Business One Version: 10.0
  • Three A/P Invoices were posted on 10-May, 21-May, and 25-May.
  • The total invoice value is approximately ₹66 Lakhs.
  • All three A/P Invoices were posted with Withholding Tax (TDS).
  • Later, the company created a Vendor Down Payment Request for approximately ₹1.14 Crores and made the payment through Outgoing Payment by selecting the Down Payment Request.
  • Now the vendor has requested that the previous A/P Invoices be adjusted/reconciled against this Down Payment.

Issue:
When attempting to perform the internal reconciliation, SAP Business One displays the following error:

You can only link to the section of the document which has no withholding tax.

It appears that SAP does not allow reconciliation because the A/P Invoices contain Withholding Tax, whereas the Down Payment was paid without Withholding Tax.

Questions:

  1. Is this standard SAP Business One behavior?
  2. Is there any supported method to reconcile these documents without cancelling and recreating them?
  3. Are there any SAP Notes or best practices addressing this scenario?
  4. Has anyone encountered a similar issue, and if so, what was the resolution?

Any guidance or suggestions would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Triggering SAP_COM_0682 Service
entry.content:
Hello,

System triggers this service when there is data in the information container. Document items should be marked before the payment run. So, the system can add document details to the information container with the payment run. To do so, below steps should be followed;

  1. "104798 - Make Settings for Information Container Categories" configuration step should be configured for "0003 - Payment Run" and "05 - SOAP API".
  2. Create a communication arrangement for "SAP_COM_0682" communication scenario.
  3. To add related FI-CA document items to the information container, each document item's, "Payment Info (DFKKOP-INFPA)" field should be filled with value "01 - Outbound Call of Web API". This field marks the item for information container.
  4. Schedule the payment run job from the "F3529 - Payment Run / Debit Memo Run" application. When job has completed successfully, system adds automatically the marked document item details to the information container. Added data could be displayed from the "FPINFCO2 - Display Information Container" application.
  5. A job should be scheduled from the "F5792  - Forward Data from Information Container" application to trigger outbound SOAP service of the SAP_COM_0682 service.

Kind regards,
Berke Günenç

entry.title: Intent Based Navigation with appSpecificRoute
entry.content:
Hi Colleagues,

We are developing navigation out of a Joule Capability, using intent based navigation. Our goal is to target an application with a specified object open already, ie, internal app navigation.

The target URL would be such as:
#IBPApplicationLog-display&/LogDetails/9FB4E95040ED6EF01900EADA8D2895A4

However, the documentation of Card Navigation Actions or navigation action handling do not mention anything about the app specific route parameter handling.

If i check the Navigation services API, i can easily achieve the goal by doing the following in the console:
oNavService = sap.ushell.Container.getService("Navigation"); 
...

oNavService .navigate({
target: {
semanticObject: "IBPApplicationLog",
action: "display",
},
appSpecificRoute: "LogDetails/9FB4E95040ED6EF01900EADA8D2895A4"
})
 
But we aren't sure how to pass such parameters, since the documentation only describes ibnTarget and ibnParams. Is there any description that tells us the syntax necessary to achieve our target url navigation?
 
Thank You and Best Regards,
Mihaly

entry.title: SAP Business One – Set Attachment Browse Dialog to Open Last Browsed Folder Per User
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I have a query regarding the Attachment functionality in SAP Business One.

Our client uses attachments extensively, with more than 50 users working in SAP Business One. Previously, when users clicked the Browse button in the Attachment window, the file dialog automatically opened the last folder that particular user had browsed, which made attaching documents very convenient.

However, after a recent change, the Browse dialog now always opens the standard/default folder instead of the user's last browsed location.

Since each user stores documents in different folders, they now have to navigate to their respective folders every time they attach a file, which affects productivity.

Is there any standard SAP Business One setting, configuration, or parameter that allows the Browse dialog to remember and open the last browsed folder for each user?

We are looking for a standard solution without any customization, if possible.

Any suggestions or guidance would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: SAP Business One AddOnIdentifier property distribution
entry.content:
Hi,
Yes, with an Implementation Add-On Identifier you'll need to recompile for every client. That identifier gets generated on the customer's own network, using their implementation license, so it's tied specifically to that network and won't run anywhere else. So if you've got multiple clients, you're repeating that whole generate-and-recompile process for each one. Not great if you're planning to distribute this more broadly.
What you actually want for distribution is a Solution Add-On Identifier. To get one, you first need to request a BASIS license from SAP, by raising a message under the SBO-SDK-AA component, giving them your partner number and the solution's name, and letting them know whether it's for one specific customer or meant to be sold to multiple. Once SAP issues the BASIS license, you generate the Solution Identifier, embed it in your AddOnIdentifier property in code, and compile once. That same build can then ship to all your clients without any further recompiling.
On the three types you asked about:
Development is just for your own dev and testing while you're building the addon, not for handing off to customers.
Implementation is generated per client using their license, and you do need to recompile each time.
Solution needs a BASIS license from SAP up front, but once you have it, one compiled addon works across all your clients.

So bottom line, if you're shipping this to multiple customers, go with Solution instead of Implementation.

entry.title: Re: Help regarding SAP Business One integration
entry.content:
Hi,

You're using the wrong tool for what you're building. The Extension Registration Data Generator (.ard) is for the new lightweight/browser client, not for classic COM-based UI automation, that's why it shows incompatible in the regular client and only shows up under lightweight extensions in the browser. For your case, skip that tool entirely. Just register and run your exe as a classic add-on via Administration, Add-ons, Add-on Manager in the regular fat client.

On connection: when SAP launches your exe as a registered add-on, it passes you a connection string via command line, you read that and call SboGuiApi.Connect with that string. That's the proper production way. The fixed debug connection string you see in SDK samples only works for attaching to an already-running client during development and testing, SAP doesn't recommend using it in production.
AddonIdentifier is unrelated to connecting, it's just for SAP's license service to recognize your add-on as a distinct product. You set it after connecting, not before.
So bottom line: drop the lightweight extension approach, build and register as a classic add-on, use the SAP-provided connection string at runtime, and set AddonIdentifier once you're past pure dev and testing.

entry.title: SAP BRIM Flow - Order to Cash (O2C)
entry.content:
Wassup Community!

I'm currently learning SAP BRIM and I'm looking for a detailed understanding of the complete Order-to-Cash (O2C) process in SAP BRIM from both a functional and technical perspective.

Most resources explain the process at a high level, but I'm trying to understand what happens internally between each BRIM component.

Specifically, I'm looking for clarification on the following:

  1. The complete end-to-end data flow starting from:
    • Product Catalog
    • Customer Subscription
    • Subscription Order
    • Fulfillment/Provisioning
    • Subscription Contract
    • Usage Collection
    • Charging
    • Billing
    • Invoicing
    • FI-CA
    • Revenue Recognition (RAR)
    • General Ledger posting
  2. The role and responsibility of each BRIM component:
    • Subscription Order Management (SOM)
    • Convergent Mediation (CM)
    • Convergent Charging (CC)
    • Convergent Invoicing (CI)
    • FI-CA
    • Revenue Accounting and Reporting (RAR)
  3. What master data and transactional data are maintained in each module?
  4. How does data flow between the modules?
    • Which objects are exchanged?
    • Which APIs, services, events, or integration mechanisms are used?
    • How are the systems integrated (CPI, RFC, SOAP, OData, etc.)?
  5. I would also like to understand the purpose of each business object in the flow, such as:
    • Subscription Order
    • Subscription Contract
    • Provider Contract
    • Consumption Item
    • Charged Item
    • Billable Item (BIT)
    • Billing Document
    • Invoice Document
    • Open Item
  6. I'm particularly interested in SAP Convergent Charging (CC):
    • What role does CC play in the overall BRIM process?
    • Is CC mandatory in every BRIM implementation, or can BRIM function without it?
    • In which business scenarios is CC required, and when can it be omitted?
  7. If possible, I'd appreciate a detailed explanation of the communication and integration between modules, including what triggers each step, what data is passed, and how the process progresses from one module to the next.

I'm trying to gain a deep understanding of the entire BRIM architecture rather than just a high-level overview.

entry.title: Re: Cannot use country group in manage allocation
entry.content:
Based on your screenshots and the behavior of Manage Allocations, the issue is not a bug. It is because Country/Region Group is not supported as a receiver group directly from Manage Global Hierarchies.

Root Cause:

The Group field in Manage Allocations does not read every hierarchy created in Manage Global Hierarchies.It only displays hierarchies that are:

  • Assigned to a supported Field Group

  • Of a supported hierarchy type for Universal Allocation

  • Registered in CBC/SSCUI for Allocation

Your hierarchy ZCOUN is active, but it has not been registered as an Allocation Field Group, so the value help remains empty.

Step 1

Check whether Country/Region supports grouping in Universal Allocation.According to the SAP standard configuration, Country/Region is generally not a supported grouping characteristic for Allocation in S/4HANA Cloud. If this is the case, you cannot use a custom country hierarchy in the Group field.Instead, use:

  • Single values

  • From–To ranges

Step 2 (If Custom Groups Are Required)

If you need to allocate by country groups (e.g., ASEAN, Europe, APAC), create a custom dimension or use an existing supported grouping characteristic such as:

  • Profit Center Group

  • Cost Center Group

  • Segment Group

  • Functional Area Group

Then derive Country into that characteristic using substitution or derivation logic.

Step 3 (Check CBC Configuration)

Verify whether a CBC activity exists to assign the hierarchy to Allocation.Go to:

Central Business Configuration

↓

Finance

↓

Universal Allocation

↓

Field Groups

 

or search for:

  • Define Field Groups
  • Assign Hierarchy Type
  • Universal Allocation Settings

If Country/Region is not listed there, SAP does not support it.

 

Solution 4 (SAP Restriction)

If your business requirement is:

 

Country Group

Thailand

Myanmar

Vietnam

 

and you want to select Thailand + Myanmar + Vietnam as one receiver group in Manage Allocation, this is currently not supported in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition unless SAP has explicitly enabled Country/Region as a group-enabled characteristic in your release.

 

If You Believe Country/Region Should Be Supported

If your project documentation or SAP release notes indicate that Country/Region groups should work:

  1. Check What's New Viewer for your S/4HANA Cloud release.
  2. Search SAP Notes using keywords:
    • "Manage Allocation Country Group"
    • "Universal Allocation Country Region Group"
    • "Manage Global Hierarchies Allocation"

 

Based on the screenshots, I am 90% confident this is a standard SAP limitation rather than a configuration error. The quickest way to verify is the Profit Center Group test described above. If that works while Country/Region does not, it confirms that Country/Region groups are simply not supported in your current S/4HANA Cloud release.

entry.title: Re: How to Set Automatically “Print via Output Control" i...
entry.content:
When an Inbound Delivery is involved, SAP treats the Goods Receipt as delivery-based GR.

In this scenario, MIGO does not determine the print indicator from OMBR/NDR.

Instead, printing is controlled by:

  • Output Determination
  • Inbound Delivery
  • Warehouse process (EWM)
  • Material Document print control

So OMBR only works for the classic MM Goods Receipt process.

entry.title: 🇸🇬 Build Code Based AI Agents on SAP Business Technology Platform (Singapore)
entry.content:
In this CodeJam, you will learn how to build state-of-the-art AI agents using the Generative AI Hub and Python. You will also gain the skills to create custom tools for your agents, including leveraging SAP's RPT-1 model. Finally, you will deploy your agents to BTP.

In this CodeJam you can choose to build your agents with Python and CrewAI or with JavaScript and LangGraph.

The CodeJam will include the following SAP technologies:

- Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core

- SAP RPT-1

- Grounding Service

- SAP AI Launchpad

- Business Application Studio (Python / JavaScript)

Exercise Content:  https://github.com/SAP-samples/codejam-code-based-agents

Prerequisites: 

  • Bring your own laptop with power adapter and have a supported Chromium-based browser
  • It can be helpful to bring a tablet or second laptop, if you have one, to have the instructions on a separate screen.

Location and Date:

This is a free in-person event and is planned for Thursday, August 27, in Singapore from 10:00 AM to 4:00 PM local time. The event's language and content will be in English. 

Event Address: SAP Asia Pte Ltd.30 Pasir Panjang Rd, Singapore 117440, Located in: Mapletree Business City.

Google Maps: https://maps.app.goo.gl/6ZEMBPXsthyshYH2A

  • A huge thank you to Mark9 Solutions for hosting this event.
  • The instructors are SAP Developer Advocates @ajay_soreng  and @shrinivasan_neelamegam 

Please note that spaces are limited! We can only accommodate 35 attendees. If you want to attend, register by clicking the Yes button in the Will you be attending? section above to the right. Approximately 2 weeks prior to the event, you will receive an email to confirm your attendance, along with further instructions and details.

entry.title: What’s new in Mobile development kit client 26.6
entry.content:
Dear Community,

I'm happy to announce that a new release of the Mobile development kit (MDK) is now available for all SAP Build and Mobile Services customers. You can download it from the SAP Software Center, or soon from the community download page.

The SAP Mobile Services Client has been updated to version 26.6.0 on the Google Play Store and will soon be updated on the Apple App Store.

SAP MDK enables developers and technical business users to build cross-platform mobile applications. It allows you to build your application once, in an integrated development environment (SAP Business Application Studio / Visual Studio Code) and run it natively on Mobile (iOS & Android).  

The following UI enhancements are available only on iOS and Android platforms:

  • Security & Compliance Enhancements
    • Pre-Onboarding Device Compliance Check
    • App Attestation Support
  • Embedded AI Enhancements
    • AI Notice Form Cell Control
    • AI Feedback Form Cell Control
  • UI Enhancements
    • Stepper Form Cell Control
    • Enhanced Offline OData Progress Feedback
    • Configurable Onboarding Illustration Sizes for Android
    • New Section Filter Bar with Advanced Filtering
    • Action Bar Image Icon Size Customization
  • Client API Enhancements
    • Dynamic Visibility and Enablement for Button Table Buttons

Pre-Onboarding Device Compliance Check

MDK already provides a device compliance check API which can be used in the application metadata after a user completes onboarding. With this release, MDK extends this capability to perform compliance checks before onboarding begins.

Key Benefits:

  • Early detection of potentially compromised devices (for example, rooted or jailbroken devices).
  • Reduced risk of exposing user credentials and corporate data to non-compliant devices.
  • Stronger security posture through compliance validation at the earliest stage of user access.
  • Flexible administrator controls to determine how non-compliant devices are handled.
  • Support for organizational security policies through configurable actions such as Block or Warn.
  • Built-in test mode for validating compliance-check behavior without requiring an actual rooted or jailbroken device.

// Excerpt of BrandedSettings.json
"PreLoginDeviceComplianceCheck": {
    "Enabled": true,
    "FailureAction": "Warn"
}​
  • The FailureAction setting determines what happens when a compromised device is detected:
    • Block – The user is shown a message indicating that the device does not meet security requirements and cannot continue with onboarding.
    • Warn – The user is shown a warning message but can choose to continue with onboarding after acknowledging the alert.

A Customized message in case of blocking users access the app.

 

A Customized message in case of warning users and continuing using the app.


For more information, refer to this documentation.

App Attestation Support

MDK now supports App Attestation, allowing SAP Mobile Services to validate that requests originate from an approved app running on a trusted device. This capability provides an additional layer of protection against app impersonation and unauthorized modifications.

Key Benefits:

  • Reduces the risk of requests originating from altered or cloned app installations.
  • Provides a reliable way for mobile services to establish trust in the source of incoming requests.
  • Supports enterprise security and compliance initiatives.
  • Works with existing MDK applications with minimal configuration changes.

App Attestation is configured through the Attestation property in the MDKProject.json file. The create-client tool automatically applies the required platform-specific settings during client generation.

//Excerpt of MDKProject.json
{
  "AppName": "YourAppName",
  "BundleID": "com.example.yourapp",
  "AppVersion": "1.0.0",
  "Attestation": {
    "iOS": {
      "Environment": "development",
      "DebugToken": "your-ios-debug-token-here"
    },
    "Android": {
      "DebugToken": "your-android-debug-token-here"
    }
  }
}

Notes:

  • Debug tokens are intended for development and testing only and are not recommended for production deployments.
  • Before configuring attestation in your MDK project, an administrator must enable and configure the Attestation feature in the mobile services admin UI. See configuration settings for iOS and Android.

    For more information, refer to this documentation.

AI Notice Form Cell Control

As part of ongoing enhancements in embedded AI capabilities, a new FormCell control, AINoticeFormCell, is introduced to display an inline notice for AI-generated content that may require review before use. 

Key Benefits:

  • Clearly indicates when content is generated using AI.
  • Helps users understand that AI-generated content may need validation.
  • Supports optional links for additional context or guidance.

The notice shows an AI icon, a customizable message, and an optional hyperlink. The hyperlink can be configured to open supporting information or trigger an MDK action when selected. A common use case is providing details about the AI feature, including usage guidelines, privacy information, or related policies.

//Excerpt of a .page
{
  "_Name": "ainotice_with_hyperlink",
  "_Type": "Control.Type.FormCell.AINotice",
  "IsVisible": true,
  "Separator": true,
  "Message": "This is an AI generated message, proceed with caution.",
  "HyperlinkText": "Learn more",
  "OnHyperlinkPress": "/MDK266/Actions/Message.action",
  "Styles": {
    "Background": "AINoticeYellowBackground",
    "Message": "AINoticeHeadlineBold"
  }
},

The AINoticeFormCellProxy class enables dynamic updates at runtime, including changes to the message, hyperlink text, styling, and click behavior through MDK rules.

let control = context.evaluateTargetPathForAPI('#Page:AINoticeClientAPI/#Control:ainotice_api_target');
  let message = control.getMessage();
   control.setMessage('Updated via Client API!');
   let hyperlinkText = control.getHyperlinkText();
     control.setHyperlinkText('Updated hyperlink');
       control.setStyles({
    "Background": "AINoticeYellowBackground",
    "Message": "AINoticeBlueMessage"
  });

AI Notice FormCell control examples
For more information, refer to this documentation.

AI Feedback Form Cell Control

As part of the embedded AI enhancement area, a new FormCell control, AIFeedback, is introduced to enable quick user feedback on AI-generated content. The control provides thumbs-up and thumbs-down options along with a caption and is designed specifically for AI-related experiences.

Key Benefits:

  • Enables quick user feedback on AI-generated content
  • Captures user sentiment in a simple, lightweight way
  • Helps improve AI experiences through user input
//Excerpt of a .page
{
  "_Type": "Control.Type.FormCell.AIFeedback",
  "_Name": "SectionFunc_InitUp",
  "Caption": "Initial value: Up",
  "Value": "Up",
  "IsEditable": true,
  "IsVisible": true,
  "Separator": true,
  "Styles": {
    "Background": "AIFeedbackBackground",
    "Caption": "AIFeedbackCaption",
    "VoteButtons": "AIFeedbackVoteButton"
  }
},

Note: This control does not provide any AI capabilities. It is a UI component used to capture feedback in AI-enabled scenarios, and developers determine what to do with the feedback and where to store it for their application.

The AIFeedbackFormCellProxy class enables dynamic updates at runtime, including changes to the caption, vote value, and styles through MDK rules.

AI Feedback Form cell control examples

For more information, refer to this documentation.

Stepper Form Cell Control

A new FormCell control, Stepper, is introduced to allow users to increase or decrease numeric values using buttons or direct input. The control supports configurable step size, value range limits, decimal precision, and validation.

Key Benefits:

  • Enables easy adjustment of numeric values through stepper buttons or manual entry
  • Supports configurable increments for flexible use cases
  • Allows enforcement of minimum and maximum value limits
  • Provides control over numeric precision for consistent input formatting
{
  "Caption": "Initial Value 5 and Step 1",
  "Value": 5,
  "DecimalPlacesSupported": 2,
  "Step": 1,
  "_Type": "Control.Type.FormCell.Stepper",
  "_Name": "StepperWithValue"
}

The StepperFormCellProxy class enables dynamic updates at runtime, including retrieves or modifies the Stepper value through MDK rules. 

Fiori Stepper Form cell control with its OnValueChange event

For more information, refer to this documentation.

Enhanced Offline OData Progress Feedback

Offline OData operations now provide step-level progress messages, offering improved visibility into long-running tasks such as initialization, download, upload, and send store. Localized default messages are displayed automatically with no additional configuration.

Key Benefits:

  • Introduces step-level progress updates for offline operations
  • Improves visibility into long-running processes such as sync and data transfer
  • Provides localized default messages out of the box
  • Enables customization and runtime control of progress feedback
  • Progress messages can be customized using the ProgressMessages property under OfflineOptions in the .service definition. These messages are used during offline operations including initialization, download, upload, and send store.
//.service file
{
  "DestinationName": "com.sap.edm.sampleservice.v4",
  "OfflineEnabled": true,
  "OfflineOptions": {
    "AutoIncludeProgressMessages": false,
    "ProgressMessages": {
      "BuildingEntityStore": "{0}/{1} Custom Building the entity store",
      "CreatingBootStrapDatabase": "{0}/{1} Custom Creating bootstrap database",
      "CreatingRequestQueueDatabase": "{0}/{1} Custom Creating request queue database",
      "DownloadingEntityStore": "{0}/{1} Custom Downloading entity store {2}",
      "ErasingExpiredRequests": "{0}/{1} Custom Erasing expired requests",
      "LoadingMetadata": "{0}/{1} Custom Performing request queue optimizer",
      "PerformingTransactionMerge": "{0}/{1} Custom Performing transaction builder",
      "PerformingCreateDeleteMerge": "{0}/{1} Custom Performing undo local creation optimizer",
      "ProcessingDefiningQueries": "{0}/{1} Custom Processing defining queries {2}",
      "ProcessingRequests": "{0}/{1} Custom Processing requests {2}",
      "ReapplyingChanges": "{0}/{1} Custom Reapplying changes",
      "AnalyzingReceivedData": "{0}/{1} Custom Receiving data from the client",
      "ReceivingDataFromServer": "{0}/{1} Custom Receiving data from the server {2}",
      "RemovingDeletedRelationships": "{0}/{1} Custom Removing deleted relationships",
      "RemovingRemoveAfterUploadRequests": "{0}/{1} Custom Removing RemoveAfterUpload requests",
      "SendingDataToServer": "{0}/{1} Custom Sending data to the server {2}",
      "SendingEntityStoreDatabase": "{0}/{1} Custom Sending entity store database {2}/{3}",
      "SendingRequestQueueDatabase": "{0}/{1} Custom Sending request queue database",
      "UploadPreprocessing": "{0}/{1} Custom Pre-processing requests before uploading",
      "WaitingForDownload": "{0}/{1} Custom Waiting for download"
    }
  },
  "OnOfflineProgressUpdate": "/MDK266/Rules/ProgressFeedback/OnOfflineProgressPromiseUpdate.js"
}

Notes: 

  • To control automatic UI behavior, use the AutoIncludeProgressMessages property.
  • A new OnOfflineProgressUpdate rule callback allows applications to  customize the progress messages at runtime. Structured event details can be accessed using the getEventData() client API.
  • ProgressText in the Initialize action is deprecated as of MDK 26.6.0. Use the new ProgressMessages instead. If both properties are configured, ProgressMessages takes precedence.

Granular progress feedback for Offline OData Initialization action

For more information, refer to this documentation.

Configurable Onboarding Illustration Sizes for Android

This enhancement is particularly useful on Android devices with limited screen space. For example, on smaller devices, onboarding illustrations can reduce the visible area available for important content such as passcode instructions and client policies. Adjusting the illustration size—or hiding it entirely—helps ensure critical information remains accessible and easier to read.

A new onboardingIllustrationSize property is available under OnboardingCustomizations in BrandedSettings.json, allowing you to control the size of illustrations displayed on onboarding screens or hide them entirely.

//Excerpt of BrandedSettings.json
"OnboardingCustomizations": {
  "onboardingIllustrationSize": "L", // XS, S, M, L, XL, None
},

This property is supported on Android for the following onboarding screens:

  • Launch
  • Set Passcode
  • Confirm Passcode
  • Notification Permission
  • Camera Permission
  • Offline Network Error

Key Benefits

  • Provides greater flexibility when designing onboarding experiences.
  • Helps optimize screen space on smaller Android devices.
  • Improves visibility of onboarding content and client policies.
  • Allows illustrations to be resized or removed based on branding and usability requirements.

    Examples showcasing onboarding illustration on Create passcode screen


    For more information, refer to this documentation.

New Section Filter Bar with Advanced Filtering

MDK now provides a new FilterBar experience for sectioned tables, enabling more advanced filtering scenarios while following SAP Fiori design patterns.

While the existing Filter Feedback Bar remains ideal for quick-access filters and common filtering actions, the new FilterBar supports richer interactions for scenarios that require more sophisticated input. Users can work with controls such as segmented selections, list pickers, switches, sliders, and multi-sort options directly within the filtering experience.

Key Benefits

  • Supports more advanced filtering scenarios beyond simple filter chips.
  • Provides a consistent experience aligned with SAP Fiori design guidelines.
  • Reduces the need for custom filtering screens and dialogs.
  • Enables users to refine large datasets more efficiently.
  • Supports a variety of filter interaction patterns, including selection lists, ranges, toggles, and sorting options.

Filter bar at one or more sections

 

//Excerpt of a .page
{
  "Sections": [
    {
      "FilterBar": {
        "_Type": "Control.Type.FilterBar",
        "_Name": "SortFilter",
        "Filters": "/MDK266/Rules/FilterBar/InitialFilters.js",
        "Sections": [
          {
            "_Name": "FilterSection1",
            "Controls": [
              {
                "_Name": "Width",
                "_Type": "FilterBar.Type.Segmented",
                "Label": "DimensionWidth Compare",
                "Items": [
                  {
                    "DisplayValue": "< 25",
                    "ReturnValue": "DimensionWidth lt 25"
                  }
                ],
                "AllowEmptySelection": true,
                "AllowMultipleSelection": false,
                "DisplayOnBar": "/MDK266/Rules/FilterBar/GetDisplayOnBarValue.js"
              },
              {
                "_Name": "PriceLP",
                "_Type": "FilterBar.Type.ListPicker",
                "Label": "Price >=",
                "Value": "Price ge 126",
                "PickerItems": {
                  "DisplayValue": "{Price}",
                  "ReturnValue": "/MDK266/Rules/FilterBar/PriceReturnValue.js",
                  "Target": {
                    "EntitySet": "Products",
                    "Service": "/MDK266/Services/SampleServiceV4Online.service",
                    "QueryOptions": "$top=30"
                  }
                },
                "DisplayOnBar": "/MDK266/Rules/FilterBar/GetDisplayOnBarValue.js"
              },
              {
                "_Name": "Favorite",
                "_Type": "FilterBar.Type.Switch",
                "Label": "Is Favorite?",
                "HelperText": "Favorite is",
                "_SimpleQuery": {
                  "Property": "Category"
                },
                "DisplayOnBar": "/MDK266/Rules/FilterBar/GetDisplayOnBarValue.js"
              },
              {
                "_Name": "Height",
                "_Type": "FilterBar.Type.Slider",
                "Label": "Height >=",
                "MinValue": 10,
                "MaxValue": 111,
                "Step": 1,
                "SimpleQuery": {
                  "Property": "DimensionHeight",
                  "Operator": "ge"
                },
                "DisplayOnBar": "/MDK266/Rules/FilterBar/GetDisplayOnBarValue.js"
              }
            ]
          }
        ]
      },
      "_Name": "Section",
      "_Type": "Section.Type.ObjectTable"
    }
  ],

Notes: 

  • This new FilterBar is defined at the section level which is more intuitive.

  • If there is only one section, the FilterBar will remain sticky (i.e., it will stay at the top of the screen while scrolling). If there are multiple sections, it will scroll with the content. Users can also define different FilterBars for individual sections, unlike the existing FilterFeedbackBar. 
    Additionally, both approaches cannot coexist on the same page; FilterBar will take higher priority.
     
    For more information, refer to this documentation.

Action Bar Image Icon Size Customization

You can now control the display size of image icons in the Action Bar using the new Width property on ActionBarItem. By specifying a width value, image icons can be displayed at a custom size instead of the default 18 × 18 dimensions.

Key Benefits:

  • Provides greater control over Action Bar appearance.
  • Supports larger image icons for improved visibility.
  • Enables more flexibility when implementing custom branding and designs.

    Action Bar item Width property examples

     

"ActionBar": {
  "Caption": "Icon Width Examples",
  "Items": [
    {
      "_Name": "IconDefault",
      "Position": "Right",
      "Icon": "/MDK266/Images/actionbar.png",
      "OnPress": "/MDK266/Actions/Message.action"
    },
    {
      "_Name": "Icon24",
      "Position": "Right",
      "Icon": "/MDK266/Images/actionbar.png",
      "Width": 24,
      "OnPress": "/MDK266/Actions/Message.action"
    },
    {
      "_Name": "Icon36",
      "Position": "Right",
      "Icon": "/MDK266/Images/actionbar.png",
      "Width": 36
    }
  ]
},​

Notes:

  • The maximum supported value is 36; values above this limit are automatically set to 36.
  • Values less than or equal to 0 use the default size of 18.
  • This property applies only to image icons and does not affect SAP icons or font icons.
  • When sizing your image, pay attention to any existing white space bordering the image.

For more information, refer to this documentation.

Dynamic Visibility and Enablement for Button Table Buttons

The ButtonTableButtonProxy now supports new client APIs that allow applications to read and dynamically control the enabled state and visibility of Button Table buttons at runtime.

Key Benefits:

  • Enables dynamic control of button availability and visibility.
  • Supports context-aware user experiences.
  • Allows button states to be updated through rules based on application logic.
  • Provides APIs to both read and modify button state at runtime.

Section Button visibility and Enablement

 

Available APIs:

  • setEnabled() — Enables or disables the button.
  • setVisible() — Shows or hides the button.
  • getEnabled() — Returns the current enabled state.
  • getVisible() — Returns the current visibility state.
export default function ToggleButtonEnabled(context) {
  let pageProxy = context.getPageProxy();
  let button = pageProxy.getControl('SectionedTable').getSection('TargetSection').getButton('ButtonTableTypeButton0');
  if (button) {
    let currentEnabled = button.getEnabled();
    button.setEnabled(!currentEnabled);
    let currentVisible = button.getVisible();
    button.setVisible(!currentVisible);
  }

Become an SAP MDK Explorer!
Do you have a requirement to build a mobile solution for your enterprise? Take your first steps today by following these tutorials to get started and learn more about the SAP Mobile development kit. We’re excited to see what you build—your feedback and ideas are invaluable!

Submit Improvement Requests
You can submit your improvement request here for Mobile development kit to the SAP Customer Influence site, a central place for all product improvement requests. You can browse all improvement requests, submit improvement requests, comment, vote, receive updates, and see who has voted at the SAP Customer Influence site.

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud | CO11N Goods Movement Validation via BAdI
entry.content:
I'm working on a tricky ABAP extensibility challenge in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud and would love inputs from anyone who has tackled something similar.

📌 The Business Problem

During Production Order confirmation (CO11N), SAP allows users to freely manipulate the goods movement data before posting - changing storage locations, modifying quantities, adding new components, or outright deleting existing BOM component lines. This is causing serious data integrity issues between our Production Order BOM, reservations, and actual goods movements.

We needed strict validation: what gets posted must exactly match the original BOM and reservation - no exceptions.

What We've Built So Far

We implemented custom validation logic across BAdIs:

✅ Rule 1: Block SLoc change → Working fine in
BADI_MMIM_CHECK_MATDOC_ITEM.
✅ Rule 2a: if Quantity less and great than equal → Working fine.
✅ Rule 3: Block unauthorised component addition → Working fine.

⚠️ Rule 2b: Block quantity change vs. reservation → Partially working (greater/less than caught, but Zero/blank not)

❌ Rule 4: Block component line deletion → NOT implemented yet
This is the hardest one. Since deleted lines never even trigger the per-item BAdI.

What I'm Looking For


Has anyone implemented deletion detection or if quantity zero & blank using BADI_MMIM_CHECK_MATDOC_ITEM in Public Cloud?
Any experience comparing BOM/reservation components against posted movement items at ITEM level?
Any gotchas with I_ProductionOrderComponent or I_ReservationDocumentItem in this context?
Best practices for raising clean error messages from ITEM-level BAdIs in Public Cloud.?

All of this is in S/4HANA Public Cloud ABAP.

If you've tried something similar- whether it worked or not- I'd love to hear about it. Every experience helps.

entry.title: 🇮🇳 Build Code-based AI Agents on SAP Business AI Platform (Bengaluru)
entry.content:
In this CodeJam, you will learn how to build state-of-the-art AI agents using the Generative AI Hub and Python. You will also gain the skills to create custom tools for your agents, including leveraging SAP's RPT-1 model. Finally, you will deploy your agents to SAP BTP.

In this CodeJam you will build your agents with Python and CrewAI.

The CodeJam will include the following SAP technologies:

- Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core

- SAP RPT-1

- Grounding Service

- SAP AI Launchpad

- Business Application Studio (Python)

Exercise Content:  https://github.com/SAP-samples/codejam-code-based-agents

Prerequisites: 

  • Bring your own laptop with power adapter and have a supported Chromium-based browser
  • It can be helpful to bring a tablet or second laptop, if you have one, to have the instructions on a separate screen.

Location and Date:

This is a free in-person event and is planned for Wednesday, August 19, in Bengaluru from 9:30 AM to 3:30 PM local time. The event's language and content will be in English. 

Event Address:

Bosch Global Software Technologies
360° Business Park, Electronic City Phase I, Electronic City, Bengaluru, Doddathoguru, Karnataka 560100

Google Maps: https://maps.app.goo.gl/18xB6jTQbUoyEMhp8

  • The instructors are SAP Developer Advocates @Shilpa_Shankar @Rekha_DR @sheenamk 

Please note that spaces are limited! We can only accommodate 35 attendees. If you want to attend, register by clicking the Yes button in the Will you be attending? section above to the right. entry.title: How We Built an Audit and Compliance Solution for SAP SuccessFactors HCM Using SAP BTP and Joule
entry.content:
Introduction
In most organizations using SAP SuccessFactors HCM, configuration changes and employee data updates happen every day. New fields are added, business rules are modified, permissions are updated, and employee records are continuously maintained across multiple system environments.

During our work with SAP SuccessFactors implementations, we frequently observed how difficult it was for administrators and compliance teams to maintain visibility into these changes. Organizations often operate multiple environments such as Development, Test, and Production, and even small configuration differences between them can lead to unexpected issues, failed deployments, or inconsistent business processes.

At the same time, tracking employee data changes is not always straightforward. Understanding who modified a record, when the change occurred, what values were updated, or whether a record was deleted often requires navigating multiple modules and reports. Preparing for audits or compliance reviews can quickly become a time-consuming and manual process.

We also noticed that maintaining configuration documentation was another major challenge. As systems evolve, documentation often becomes outdated, making it difficult for teams to validate configurations, troubleshoot issues, or ensure compliance with organizational standards.

The real challenge was not simply collecting audit information—it was providing a centralized and intelligent way to compare configurations, track changes, monitor compliance activities, and generate accurate documentation across the entire SAP SuccessFactors landscape.

This realization led us to build an Audit and Compliance Solution for SAP SuccessFactors HCM using SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) and SAP Joule. The solution was designed to help organizations improve governance, strengthen compliance, reduce manual effort, and gain complete visibility into both configuration and employee data changes.

Our Approach
To address these challenges, we built an Audit and Compliance Solution for SAP SuccessFactors HCM on SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) and integrated it with SAP Joule to provide a centralized and intelligent auditing experience.

While designing this solution, one of our primary goals was to simplify how organizations monitor, validate, and govern their SAP SuccessFactors landscape. Instead of relying on multiple reports, manual comparisons, and fragmented audit information, we wanted to bring everything together into a single platform that provides complete visibility into system and employee-related changes.

What mattered most during development was usability and transparency. Administrators should be able to quickly identify configuration differences between environments, track employee data modifications, review permission changes, monitor recruitment activities, and generate documentation without spending hours collecting information from different sources.

We also wanted to reduce the dependency on manual audits and documentation efforts. By automating metadata comparison, workbook generation, and change tracking, the solution enables organizations to focus on analysis and decision-making rather than data collection.

With this approach, organizations can confidently maintain consistency across environments, strengthen compliance processes, improve governance, and gain complete visibility into configuration and employee data changes while significantly reducing operational effort.

Enhancing the Experience with SAP Joule

While the Audit and Compliance Solution provides comprehensive auditing capabilities, integrating it with Joule makes accessing audit information faster and more intuitive.

Instead of navigating multiple screens or manually searching through audit reports, administrators can interact with the solution using natural language. Joule interprets user requests and helps retrieve relevant audit information, compare configurations, and initiate documentation generation through a conversational interface.

Some examples include:

  • Compare metadata between Development, Test, and Production environments.
  • Retrieve employee data audit history.
  • Review Role-Based Permission (RBP) changes.
  • View deleted employee records.
  • Generate configuration or business rule workbooks.
  • Access audit insights using natural language prompts.

This conversational experience reduces manual navigation, improves productivity, and enables administrators to quickly access the information required for governance, compliance, and troubleshooting

 


Key Features and Benefits

1. Metadata Comparison Across Systems

  • One of the biggest challenges organizations face is ensuring configuration consistency across Development, Test, and Production environments. Even small differences in metadata configurations can lead to unexpected behavior, deployment issues, or business process disruptions.
  • To address this, the solution provides a comprehensive metadata comparison capability that analyzes configurations across multiple SAP SuccessFactors environments. Users can quickly identify differences at both entity and field levels, including properties such as field type, required status, visibility settings, and other configuration attributes.
  • What makes this capability valuable is the ability to drill down into detailed comparison results while also providing a summarized view for faster analysis. This significantly reduces the effort required to validate configurations and troubleshoot deployment issues.

 

2. Configuration Workbook Generation

  • Maintaining configuration documentation is often a manual and time-consuming task. As SuccessFactors environments evolve, documentation can quickly become outdated, making audits and validations more difficult.
  • The solution automatically generates configuration workbooks directly from SuccessFactors metadata. Users can select specific modules, entities, and fields to create structured Excel-based documentation.
  • This ensures organizations always have access to accurate and up-to-date configuration baselines while significantly reducing manual documentation effort.

 

3. Employee Data Change Audit

  • Tracking employee data changes is a critical requirement for both compliance and operational governance. However, identifying who changed a record, what was changed, and when the change occurred often requires significant manual investigation.
  • The Employee Data Audit capability provides complete visibility into employee-related modifications by capturing change history, event details, modification timestamps, and responsible users.
  • This enables HR and compliance teams to quickly investigate changes, improve accountability, and maintain audit readiness.

 


4. Timesheet Audit

  • Timesheet data plays an important role in workforce management and payroll processes. Organizations often require visibility into timesheet updates, approvals, and historical modifications.
  • The Timesheet Audit feature provides detailed tracking of employee timesheet activities, including modification history, status updates, and audit records.
  • This helps organizations improve transparency while supporting compliance and reporting requirements.

 


5. Benefits Audit

  • Employee benefits data frequently changes throughout the employee lifecycle. Maintaining visibility into these changes is essential for compliance and operational accuracy.
  • The Benefits Audit module enables organizations to track benefit-related updates, view modification history, identify responsible users, and export audit information for reporting purposes.
  • This provides greater transparency and simplifies compliance reviews.


6. Role-Based Permission (RBP) Audit

  • Managing user permissions is one of the most critical aspects of governance and security within SAP SuccessFactors.
  • The RBP Audit capability allows organizations to monitor permission changes, review modified roles, identify responsible administrators, and track historical updates.
  • By providing complete visibility into permission-related activities, organizations can strengthen security controls and support compliance initiatives.


7. Recruitment Auditing

  • Recruitment processes involve continuous activity across job requisitions and candidate applications. Monitoring these activities is important for operational oversight and compliance reporting.
  • The solution provides dedicated auditing capabilities for both Job Applications and Job Requisitions, enabling users to track recruitment activity, review status changes, monitor modification history, and generate reports when needed.
  • This helps organizations maintain transparency throughout the recruitment lifecycle.

 


8. Deleted Records Tracking

  • Deleted employee records can create significant challenges during audits and investigations if proper tracking mechanisms are not available.
  • The solution identifies deleted records and provides details about when the deletion occurred and who performed the action. This helps organizations maintain historical visibility and supports compliance investigations when required.


9. Historical Audit Record Management

  • As audit data grows over time, organizations need efficient ways to manage historical information while complying with retention policies.
  • The solution provides capabilities to review and purge historical audit records in a controlled manner. This helps manage data volume, improve system performance, and maintain compliance with organizational retention requirements.

 

 

10. Business Rule Extraction and Workbook Generation

  • Business rules are a critical component of SuccessFactors implementations, yet documenting them manually can be challenging and error-prone.
  • The solution automatically extracts business rules from the system and generates structured workbooks for documentation and review purposes.
  • This improves documentation accuracy, simplifies governance processes, and reduces ongoing maintenance effort.

Business Value

  • This solution delivers significant value by helping organizations strengthen governance, improve compliance, and reduce manual effort associated with auditing and documentation activities.
  • One of the biggest advantages is improved visibility. Administrators and compliance teams can quickly identify configuration differences across environments, monitor employee data changes, and track critical system activities from a centralized platform.
  • The solution also improves audit readiness by providing detailed tracking of employee updates, permission changes, recruitment activities, and deleted records. Instead of spending hours collecting information from multiple sources, teams can access the required audit information in a single place.
  • Another important benefit is the reduction of manual effort. Tasks such as metadata comparison, workbook generation, business rule extraction, and audit reporting are automated, allowing teams to focus on analysis and decision-making rather than data collection.
  • By providing accurate documentation and complete change visibility, the solution helps organizations improve data quality, maintain configuration consistency, and reduce operational risks across their SAP SuccessFactors landscape.
  • Finally, because the solution is built on SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP), it offers a secure, scalable, and enterprise-ready foundation that can support organizations of all sizes.

Conclusion
Managing configurations, employee data changes, permissions, and compliance activities across SAP SuccessFactors environments can quickly become a complex and time-consuming task. As organizations grow, maintaining visibility into these activities becomes increasingly important for governance, operational efficiency, and regulatory compliance.

The Audit and Compliance Solution for SAP SuccessFactors HCM addresses these challenges by providing a centralized platform for metadata comparison, employee data auditing, recruitment tracking, permission monitoring, and automated documentation generation.

From our experience working with SAP SuccessFactors implementations, we have seen how difficult it can be to manually track changes, validate configurations, and prepare for audits. By automating these processes and bringing critical audit information together in a single platform, organizations can significantly reduce effort while improving transparency and control.

Ultimately, this solution is not just about auditing system activities—it is about enabling organizations to operate with greater confidence. By providing complete visibility into changes across the SAP SuccessFactors landscape, organizations can strengthen compliance, improve governance, and focus on driving business value rather than managing manual audit processes.

entry.title: Standard solution for Vendor Bill Back PDF generation and email distribution of FB65 posting

  2. Generate a PDF document using standard form technologies.
  3. Email the PDF automatically to the vendor using the supplier master email address.

entry.title: Filters not propagating to Data Analyzer across linked models
entry.content:
When using the "Open Data Analyzer" option from a Story that contains multiple charts based on different data models, the active filters are not propagated to the Data Analyzer. Instead, the filters are dropped, and the following warning message is displayed:

"One or more filters across models have been dropped. Only direct filters across models are taken over to data analyzer."

Steps to Reproduce

  1. Create a Story with at least two charts, where each chart depends on a different data model.
  2. Link the dimensions and variables of both models within the Story (Model Linking).
  3. Apply a story filter or a page filter that affects both models.
  4. Trigger/click the option "Open Data Analyzer..." from one of the charts or components.

Expected Behavior
The active filters applied in the Story should propagate correctly to the Data Analyzer, maintaining the context of the analyzed data even when models are linked.

Actual Behavior
Filters are dropped during the transition to Data Analyzer, and the system shows the warning message: "One or more filters across models have been dropped..."

entry.title: Re: SWPM NW 7.5 Java Install Error Code 99
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I have encountered the same issue again after a few years during an SAP NetWeaver 7.5 Java installation. Initially, I suspected that firewall or antivirus settings might be interfering, but after further investigation, I identified that the root cause is related to the Java security policy.

The issue occurs because the modern SAP JVM (8.1.x and higher) prevents the loading of the legacy IAIK JCE libraries, which are signed with SHA1. This triggers a NoEncryptionException and causes the UMConfigurator step to fail with return code 99.

Solution:

To resolve this, you need to allow SHA1 algorithms by modifying the java.security file of the JVM instance currently being used by the installer.

  1. Locate the java.security file in the JRE directory used by the SWPM process: (Usually found in: ...\sapinst_instdir\...\sapjvm_8\jre\lib\security\java.security)

  2. Open the file with a text editor and search for the property: jdk.jar.disabledAlgorithms

  3. Remove the SHA1 denyAfter 2019-01-01 part from the list. Ensure the line looks like this: jdk.jar.disabledAlgorithms=MD2, MD5, RSA keySize < 1024, DSA keySize < 1024, include jdk.disabled.namedCurves

  4. Save the file. (Tip: You may set the file to "Read-only" temporarily to prevent SWPM from overwriting it during the "Retry" process).

  5. Go back to the SWPM screen and click Retry.

Once the installation is complete, do not forget to revert these security settings or apply the necessary patches to keep your environment secure.

Hope this helps anyone else running into this classic hurdle!

Best regards,
Ismail Cerban

entry.title: Re: The relationship between TIH and the Competency Library
entry.content:
Hi Nanbu,

Good day!

Regarding the issue,

1. Do competencies added in TIH not sync with the competency library?

>>If you are using TIH, then the "center of capabilities" (JPB competency library) will not be available.
*Note: for mappings like role competency mapping etc. the JPB tools are still in use; TIH (the tool "manage talent intelligence hub") only replaces "center of capabilities" (JPB competency library).

The tool "Manage writing assistance and coach advisor" (Also may be seen as the JDM competency library) is available, but generally in TIH enabled instance it is not recommended to make changes in this tool, as TIH (manage talent intelligence hub) is the main place to create/modify all the attributes (skills, competencies, custom attributes etc.)

Also the data syncing order is : TIH > JDM

Plus, I checked in the demo instance, the competencies created in TIH are stored in the library "Talent Intelligence Hub Library" when checking in the tool "Manage writing assistance and coach advisor" (JDM competency library).

2. Also, I can assign a competency library to the competency section in a PM form template, but if the competencies added in TIH do not exist in the competency library, does that mean I cannot register the competencies added in TIH on a PM form?

>>Yes competencies created in TIH can be used in PM form. In TIH enabled instance, in manage templates when adding custom competencies to form, the competencies could be found by their "Tag". If still the competencies created in TIH can not be used in form in your instance, please consider raising a support ticket for further investigation.

Thank you!

Best regards,
Makki

entry.title: Re: Performance - Link two forms directly
entry.content:
Hi Uli,

Good day!

Regarding the issue, please refer to this 2605 new feature which may (partially?) meet your expectation:

New Rule Scenario: Launch Form Based on Assessment Outcome
When the latest experience of Performance Management forms is enabled, you can configure business rules to automatically launch follow-up forms, such as performance improvement plans, when initial forms are submitted with certain ratings. The rules can also inform users about the conditional form launches and carry some information from the initial forms into the follow-up forms.

Here are some examples of applying this rule scenario:

  • If the potential rating is "High", launch a leadership development form.
  • If the overall competency rating is "Below Expectations", launch an individual development plan form.
  • If the overall performance rating is "Below Expectations", launch a performance improvement plan form.

Below are some article references on this feature:

what's new viewer link:
New Rule Scenario: Launch Form Based on Assessment Outcome

Guide:
Creating a Business Rule to Launch Form Based on Assessment Outcome


Thank you!

Best regards,
Makki

entry.title: How to Configure SEPA Instant Transfers in SAP (DMEE / CGI Format)
entry.content:
How to Configure SEPA Instant Transfers in SAP (DMEE / CGI Format)

Tags: Payroll FI-AP DMEE SEPA BAdI CGI

The Problem

A recurring question in SAP communities and support channels: how do you make SAP generate <Cd>INST</Cd> in the payment XML so the bank processes the payment as an instant transfer?

Many customers configure the payment method in transaction FBZP and find no option to mark a transfer as "instant payment." After searching through notes and community threads, the instructions seem scattered and contradictory. This post consolidates the answer.

What needs to happen in the XML

For the bank to process a transfer as a SEPA Instant Credit Transfer (SCT Inst), the generated XML file (pain.001 format) must carry the INST indicator. The standard requires adding a <SvcLvl> (Service Level) block inside the payment information (<PmtInf>) or within each individual credit transfer (<CdtTrfTxInf>). This value tells the bank to route the order through the SCT Inst scheme rather than the standard SEPA Credit Transfer.

Option A — Service Level at payment batch level

<PmtInf>
  <PmtTpInf>
    <SvcLvl>
      <Cd>SEPA</Cd>
    </SvcLvl>
    <LclInstrm>
      <Cd>INST</Cd>
    </LclInstrm>
  </PmtTpInf>
  <!-- ... rest of payment info ... -->
</PmtInf>

Option B — Local Instrument at transaction level

<CdtTrfTxInf>
  <PmtTpInf>
    <LclInstrm>
      <Cd>INST</Cd>
    </LclInstrm>
  </PmtTpInf>
  <!-- ... rest of transaction ... -->
</CdtTrfTxInf>

The <LclInstrm><Cd>INST</Cd> node is the key signal. Banks supporting SCT Inst will detect it and apply the instant processing rules (funds available within 10 seconds, 24/7/365).

This node appears at two levels in the pain.001 structure: - Batch level (<PmtInf><PmtTpInf>) — for SEPA payments - Transaction level (<PmtInf><CdtTrfTxInf><PmtTpInf>) — for non-SEPA payments

Why it doesn't work out of the box

The CGI DMEE trees control this node through the exit function FI_CGI_DMEE_EXIT_W_BADI. If you look at the standard implementation, you'll find this code:

WHEN '<PmtInf><PmtTpInf><LclInstrm><Cd>'.
*   This node defines the Local instrument for the payment
    CLEAR c_value.

WHEN '<PmtInf><CdtTrfTxInf><PmtTpInf><LclInstrm><Cd>'.
*   This node defines the Local instrument for the payment
    CLEAR c_value.

SAP explicitly clears this node in standard for countries including ES and DE. There is no customizing switch to change this behavior — it requires a BAdI implementation.

Solution: BAdI FI_CGI_DMEE_COUNTRIES_CUST

The exit function calls two BAdIs in sequence:

BAdI Who can implement Purpose

FI_CGI_DMEE_COUNTRIESSAP onlyStandard country-specific logic
FI_CGI_DMEE_COUNTRIES_CUSTCustomerOverride values for specific nodes

The recommended approach is to keep the standard implementation intact and use the customer BAdI to override only the nodes that need modification.

Step-by-step

  1. Apply the latest CGI tree notes Implement all notes listed in the overview of SAP Note 2253571 (collective information note for CGI_XML_CT and CGI_XML_DD). The DMEE tree references exit function modules delivered by these notes — having an outdated implementation causes unexpected behavior.

  2. Create a BAdI implementation In transaction SE19 (or via SPRO), create an implementation for BAdI FI_CGI_DMEE_COUNTRIES_CUST in Enhancement Spot ES_IDFI_CGI_DMEE.

  3. Implement the method In the BAdI method, check the node path and assign the value INST when appropriate:

abap " Pseudocode — adapt to your business logic WHEN '<PmtInf><PmtTpInf><LclInstrm><Cd>'. c_value = 'INST'.

You can add conditions here to apply INST only to specific payment methods, house banks, or company codes — giving you full control over which payments are sent as instant transfers.

  1. Follow the official guide SAP Note 2253571 includes an attachment: 'Customer Enhancement in CGI with BAdI_150416.docx'. This document provides a full worked example of how to implement the customer BAdI correctly.

Alternative: PMW user exits (OBPM3 / OBPM1)

If the information needed by the bank is not available in the standard payment structures (FPAYH, FPAYP, FAPYHX), you can use Payment Medium Workbench exits to enrich the data before it reaches DMEE:

  • Event 05 (transaction OBPM1) → populates FPAYH-REFxx fields
  • Event 06 (transaction OBPM3) → populates FPAYHX-ZREFxx fields via a custom function module with the same interface as FI_PAYMEDIUM_SAMPLE_06. The ZREF fields are then mapped in the DMEEX tree.

Note: ZREF fields cannot be populated via Event 05 — use Event 06 for those.

Reference: SAP Note 965723 — PMW: use of exits (transaction OBPM3)

ECC vs S/4HANA / SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll

System CGI Tree Notes

ECC on-premiseCGI_XML_CT, CGI_XML_DDNo country-specific trees. Use BAdI CUST for all country/bank requirements.
S/4HANA on-premiseES_CGI_XML_CT (country-specific)Also available via note 2518834 / 2682253
SFECP (cloud)CGI_CT, CGI_DD (DMEEX engine)See note 2784858 and 3652001 for latest updates

Reference Notes

Note Content

2253571Collective note: CGI_XML_CT, CGI_XML_DD + BAdI enhancement guide
2241311CGI country-specific functionality for ES, IT (CBI), AT, DK, CN, TW, AU, HK, US, GB, MX, SE
3652001CGI: CGI_XML_CT, CGI_XML_DD Format Updates #49
2784858Collective note: CGI_CT, CGI_DD based on DMEEX engine
2518834Payment Formats available in S/4HANA 1709 on-premise
2682253Payment Formats supported in S/4HANA 1809 on-premise
965723PMW: use of exits (transaction OBPM3)

Summary

There is no standard customizing option to activate SEPA Instant Transfers in SAP payment files. The path is:

  1. Ensure CGI tree notes are up to date (note 2253571 overview)
  2. Implement BAdI FI_CGI_DMEE_COUNTRIES_CUST to set c_value = 'INST' for the <LclInstrm><Cd> node
  3. Add business logic conditions as needed (payment method, company code, etc.)
  4. Consult the example document in note 2253571 for implementation details

This is a consulting activity, not a software error — SAP does not plan to deliver INST as a standard default for any country. The BAdI is specifically provided for this kind of bank- and country-specific customization.

Have you implemented SEPA Instant Transfers in your system? Share your experience in the comments.

entry.title: Re: Can be maintain the OBYC table company code wise
entry.content:
If valuation is at company code level, then OBYC settings are effectively company code–specific. If valuation is at plant level, then OBYC entries are maintained per plant. OBYC is not a single table you edit directly. It is a customizing transaction that links transaction keys (e.g., BSX, GBB, WRX) with GL accounts. The determination depends on chart of accounts, valuation area, and sometimes account modifier.
Having said that, if multiple company codes share the same chart of accounts, then OBYC entries will be common across them. In that case, differentiation is only possible via valuation grouping codes or account modifiers.

entry.title: From Intent to Action with Joule Work & Knowledge Graph
entry.content:
Enterprise interaction is shifting from clicks to intent. Joule Work enables users to express what they want, while AI agents orchestrate processes, data, and applications behind the scenes. Powered by the Knowledge Graph, every action is grounded in business context and trusted data.

Join this session to explore how Joule Work works, how the Knowledge Graph enables meaningful AI, and what architects need to design—APIs, events, and semantic models—to make intent-driven enterprise a reality.

entry.title: Re: GET Parameter ID in Update-Function-Module
entry.content: You can't GET that parameter in a background session. Either store it in a custom table & SESLECT form it or EXPORT to database in the  report and IMPORT form database in the batch job.

entry.title: Build Partner Products on SAP BTP ABAP Environment and Bring Them to SAP Store
entry.content:
Do you want to offer your solution to SAP customers as a cloud product?

SAP BTP ABAP Environment enables partners to build cloud products and side-by-side extensions with ABAP Cloud on SAP BTP and bring them to market via SAP Store. These products run on SAP BTP and can integrate with SAP S/4HANA, other SAP solutions and non-SAP solutions.

To support this journey, SAP BTP ABAP Environment Product Management offers a free engagement for partners who want to build and commercialize products on SAP BTP ABAP Environment.

Who is this for?

This engagement is intended for SAP partners who:

  • want to offer a product to multiple customers
  • are planning a side-by-side solution on SAP BTP instead of an on-stack SAP S/4HANA extension
  • are considering SAP BTP ABAP Environment as their product platform
  • want to publish their solution via SAP Store

To commercialize and publish a partner product via SAP Store, an SAP PartnerEdge Build agreement is required. If you are not yet part of SAP PartnerEdge Build, we can discuss the required next steps in the initial conversation.

Supported delivery options

We currently support two delivery options for partner products on SAP BTP ABAP Environment.

Multitenant SaaS: The partner offers and operates the solution as a cloud service. Customers subscribe to the service and receive their own tenant in the partner's provider system.

Add-On Product: The partner offers the solution as software. Customers run their own SAP BTP ABAP Environment instance and consume the partner product in their own landscape, with the option to extend it according to their needs.

Both options enable partners to productize cloud solutions depending on their target customers, operating model and business model. Partners can also offer both delivery options in parallel, as most of the underlying steps are identical.

How we support you

As part of the engagement, SAP BTP ABAP Environment Product Management can support you with:

  • choosing the right delivery option
  • validating your product scenario and architecture
  • answering technical questions during implementation
  • sharing reference materials and tutorials
  • preparing your solution for SAP Store publication
  • moving towards go-live

The goal is to help partners bring their products live on SAP BTP ABAP Environment faster.

Interested?

If your company is planning to build a partner product on SAP BTP ABAP Environment, we would be happy to hear from you.

Please contact us: sap_btp_abap_environment@sap.com

To help us prepare the first discussion, please include a short description of your product idea or existing solution, your target customers, current development stage, and preferred delivery option if already known.

We will then set up a remote session to better understand your scenario and discuss how we can support your journey.

 

Helpful resources

To get started, you may find the following resources useful: 

  • Building partner products on SAP BTP ABAP Environment
  • Overview of delivery options: Multitenant SaaS and Add-On Product
  • Tutorial: MT SaaS Partner Reference Application
  • PE Build onboarding

entry.title: Utilities CX Reloaded: Discover the New Sales Process in Our July 7 Espresso Session
entry.content:
Dear Utilities Community,

We are delighted to invite you to another Espresso Session in our popular 30-minute format, where we will introduce our new Utilities CX sales process and how it helps utilities simplify and modernize sales operations in deregulated energy markets.

Discover how customer service and sales agents can initiate sales workflows directly from the SAP Sales and Service Cloud desktop, create tailored utility sales orders with relevant product recommendations, and seamlessly integrate front-office activities with SAP S/4HANA Utilities processes.

You'll also see how guided energy sales brings previously fragmented process steps together into a single, end-to-end workflow, enabling greater efficiency, faster responses to customer needs, and improved sales performance.

 

Key Topics

✅SAP Sales Cloud and Service Cloud Version 2 for Utilities
Learn about the benefits of the utilities add-on and how it supports modern utility sales processes.

✅Utility Use Cases
See how utilities can enhance sales and customer engagement through deep integration with back-end systems and business processes.

✅Live Demo
Experience a streamlined, end-to-end sales workflow in action and see how SAP solutions work together to support utility sales teams.

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Who Should Join?

This session is intended for professionals in utilities working in sales and account management, customer service and contact center leaders, CX, CRM, and business process experts, and IT and SAP architecture who want to simplify the sales processes across the organization.

 
If you are looking to simplify sales processes, improve customer interactions, and drive greater efficiency across your organization, this session is for you.
 
Our speakers Tijana Mattoo and Steen Hansen are happy to answer your questions. 
 
Please register here for our session taking place on
📅Tuesday, July 7, 2026
🕓4:00 - 4:30 PM CEST
 
We look forward to seeing you there! ☕

entry.title: Solution for Attachment Replication from SAP Business Suite to sales cloud V2
entry.content:
The standard Replicate Sales Order and Sales Quote from SAP S4HANA iFlow only covers header, products, prices, and parties "no attachments".

Is there a standard SAP iFlow/content package for replicating S/4 sales order/quote attachments to Sales Cloud V2 (similar to the old C4C AttachmentFolderReplicationRequest_Out scenario)? Or is a custom post-exit using API_CV_ATTACHMENT_SRV → V2 AttachmentFolder the only way? or there is any other way to handle this integration

Has anyone built this in a live project?

Many Thanks,

Ruchita

entry.title: Re: Compensation Promotion with Position Management Not E...
entry.content: The job must be created first. If the manager wants to create a new position, or, promote someone to a position that is already filled (i.e. you have the maximum of 4 district managers and need a 5th), the position needs to be added/updated prior to the compensation action.
